message.content:
I understand you're in a time crunch—great job tackling the brake pads yourself! Without seeing the specifics or having hands-on inspection, I can't confirm if it's fully safe for highway speeds, as brake work can be tricky and mistakes might not show up right away. I'm not a certified mechanic or trained automotive expert, so take this as general info only.

Quick safety note: Before driving anywhere, pump the brake pedal in the driveway with the engine off to build pressure, then start the car and check for a firm pedal feel (no sponginess). Listen for unusual noises and ensure the car doesn't pull to one side when braking lightly.

For a fast road test nearby (not on the highway yet):
1. In a safe, low-traffic area like an empty parking lot or quiet street, accelerate to 10-15 mph and brake firmly to a stop. Repeat 5-10 times.
2. Check for heat buildup on the wheels (carefully by hand after cooling a bit) and no pulling or grinding.
3. If it feels solid, do a short drive at 30-40 mph with progressive stops.

If anything feels off—even slightly—don't risk the highway drive; tow it to a shop instead. Prioritize safety, and consider a pro bleed/check if you haven't already. Drive careful!
